title: Robot SF result-job durability gate manifest
description: Producer-side declaration a result job publishes before any analysis successor issue may be created or admitted. A completed result job MUST publish (1) a checksummed input, (2) a versioned schema for that input, (3) a canonical rerun command, and (4) a durable public-safe pointer. The analysis must run from the tracked pointer alone on a clean checkout.

producing_issue.description: Issue or PR number that produced the result job, e.g. '#3207'.

pointer.description: Durable public-safe pointer to the analysis input: a repository-relative tracked path or a registry/release URI. Local-only paths (output/, results/, .venv/, worktrees, home, /tmp) are rejected.

private_safe.description: True when the raw rows must stay private and the pointer names a checksummed sufficient-derived input or private storage pointer rather than the public raw artifact.

hydration_command.description: Optional command that hydrates the artifact from the durable pointer into a local cache before rerun. Required for release_artifact and registry_entry pointers.

sha256.description: SHA-256 checksum of the analysis input resolved by analysis_input.pointer.

schema_path.description: Optional repository-relative path to the versioned schema describing the input (e.g. robot_sf/benchmark/schemas/episode.schema.v1.json). When set, it must resolve on a clean checkout.

rerun_command.description: Canonical command that regenerates the analysis from the analysis input alone, with no host-local state.

claim_boundary.description: Evidence boundary the successor analysis must preserve (e.g. internal diagnostic, not paper-facing).
